name: Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir South Route
name_original: Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri – circular desde Muskurio
description: This moderate 8.0-mile (12.8 km) mountain biking loop around Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir offers scenic views and birdwatching.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Barrundia, located in Spain's Basque Country, offers diverse terrain characterized by the Sierra de Elgea mountain range and its valleys. The region features limestone formations, quaternary sediments, and significant natural water bodies like the River Zadorra and the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir. These geographical elements provide varied elevation profiles and landscapes suitable for mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Barrundia The most popular mountain bike trail is Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir South Route, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail that takes about 54 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views around the reservoir and opportunities for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Urbia Plateau – Burgamendi Summit loop from Larrea, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.2 km) path. This challenging route includes significant elevation gain towards the Burgamendi Summit, providing panoramic views. Local mountain bikers also love the Rotalde Substation – Forest Ascent Trail loop from Alegría-Dulantzi, a 23.5 miles (37.9 km) trail leading through forested ascents, often completed in about 3 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Barrundia is defined by the Sierra de Elgea, the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir, and varied limestone terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult summit climbs. The routes in Barrundia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 2500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Barrundia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Barrundia?
A: Barrundia offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 100 routes documented on komoot. These range from easy paths to challenging climbs, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Barrundia?
A: The trails in Barrundia cater to all abilities. You'll find 9 easy routes, 64 moderate routes, and 33 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced riders can find suitable challenges.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that offer scenic views or pass by natural landmarks?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Barrundia feature stunning natural scenery. For panoramic views, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbia Plateau – Burgamendi Summit loop from Larrea&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends towards Burgamendi Summit. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077725380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir South Route&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic views around the reservoir and opportunities for birdwatching. The region is also home to the Sierra de Elgea mountain range and the River Zadorra, offering diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find routes that include natural water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15005568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views – Urkate Waterfall loop from Barria&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that offers views and passes by the Urkate Waterfall, providing a refreshing highlight during your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Barrundia?
A: Barrundia's terrain is quite varied, characterized by the Sierra de Elgea mountain range, limestone formations, and quaternary sediments in the valleys. You can expect a mix of climbs, descents, and flatter sections, often along rivers like the Zadorra and Barrundia, or around the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir. Some routes, particularly in the Sierra de Elgea, may traverse wind farms and reach peaks over 1100 meters.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Barrundia?
A: The mountain biking trails in Barrundia are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 2500 mountain bikers have explored the area using komoot, often praising the diverse terrain and scenic beauty.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain biking options in Barrundia?
A: While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the guide, the presence of 9 easy routes suggests there are options suitable for less experienced riders or families. The Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir area is known for its accessible routes and scenic environment, which can be enjoyable for families.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or landmarks to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Barrundia offers several natural attractions. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/837300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; or the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715462" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eye of Aitzulo&lt;/a&gt;. For those seeking elevated views, peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Urbina&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2867465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aketegi Summit&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Barrundia?
A: Barrundia experiences a transitional climate with both Atlantic and continental influences. While substantial rainfall occurs throughout the year, especially in cool winters, spring and autumn generally offer more pleasant temperatures and conditions for mountain biking. Summers can also be good, but it's always wise to check local weather forecasts.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Barrundia?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Barrundia are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077725380" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir South Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular tour around the reservoir. Other loop options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20505768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rotalde Substation – Forest Ascent Trail loop from Alegría-Dulantzi&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24637758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Indiagana – Cascina Montanina loop from Alegría-Dulantzi&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I expect to see wildlife while mountain biking in Barrundia?
A: Yes, Barrundia is known for its rich and varied wildlife. Keep an eye out for animals such as squirrels, hares, badgers, foxes, wild boars, and roe deer. The areas around the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir, particularly in Mendixur, Urizar, and Garaio, are also notable for birdlife, including nesting storks.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging options in Barrundia, with 33 difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5345972" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urbia Plateau – Burgamendi Summit loop from Larrea&lt;/a&gt; is a particularly demanding route, covering 43.2 km with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, requiring good physical fitness and advanced skills.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is described as 'circular desde Muskurio', suggesting Muskurio as a primary access point. The Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir area generally offers parking facilities, especially near popular access points like Garaio or Landa. Look for designated parking areas around the reservoir's southern end.

faqs.question: Is the Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific 12.8 km circular route is considered suitable for all fitness levels and is often highlighted as a family-friendly option. It has low difficulty and minimal elevation gain, making it ideal for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally described as a moderate mountain bike ride. However, for its shorter version (around 12.8 km), it's considered to have low technical difficulty, corresponding to STS S0 - S1 (easy to moderate), making it accessible without advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The circular route primarily follows the shore of the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir, offering a mostly flat trajectory. The terrain consists of dirt tracks and some stone sections. You will also encounter wooden walkways, particularly over wetland areas. The path is typically well-trodden and clearly signposted.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning natural landscapes of the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir. Key attractions include the Pedestrian and cycle bridge and the View of Garaio and Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir. You'll also pass through the Mendixur Ornithological Park, an important wetland for migratory birds, and enjoy shaded forested sections.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round. In autumn, the surrounding mountains display vibrant ocher and yellow hues, adding to the scenic beauty. During the summer months, visitors can also enjoy swimming in the reservoir, making it a great option for a warm-weather ride.

faqs.question: Is the Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is within a natural park area, dogs are generally permitted on trails in Parque Provincial de Garaio. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife in the Mendixur Ornithological Park, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Parque Provincial de Garaio?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Parque Provincial de Garaio or to ride the Colas de Embalse Ullíbarri route. The park is publicly accessible for recreational activities.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated biking time for this 12.8 km route is around 54 minutes. However, this can vary based on your pace, stops for photos, and enjoying the scenery. The average duration for this tour is approximately 54 minutes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of a larger network around the reservoir. It covers 100% of the "Vuelta completa al embalse Ullibarri-Gamboa" and also passes through sections of "Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea" (13%), "Ruta Norte del embalse Ullibarri-Gamboa" (7%), and "Pasarela de Azua" (7%). It also connects to other greenways leading into Vitoria-Gasteiz.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like resting areas or places to swim along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the trail, there are designated resting areas equipped with tables, perfect for a break or a picnic. During the summer months, the Ullíbarri-Gamboa reservoir is a popular spot for swimming, offering a refreshing option after your ride.

waypoints.name: View of Garaio and Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir
